note:
本文档描述了一种稳定的自适应基数/归并混合排序，名为wolfort。对于随机和有序数据的混合排序，它可能是迄今为止编写的最快的排序。
虽然自适应合并排序在排序有序数据时速度非常快，但它不能有效地进行分区是其最大的弱点。另一方面，基数排序无法利用已排序的数据。WolfSort试图避免每种算法的最坏情况。
WolfSort使用QuadSort作为其自适应合并排序，因为它比TimSort更快。Wolfort使用的基数排序有两个主要函数。
WolfSort的操作类似于典型的基数排序，方法是创建256bucket，并将每个无符号32位整数除以16777216。如果不进行任何优化，这将使内存开销增加256倍。作为一种混合排序，wolfort通过赋予每个存储桶最大大小的1/8来解决这个问题。
如果达到最大存储桶大小，则中止基数排序并运行四次排序。虽然这看起来可能很浪费，但实际上这意味着以相对较小的成本避免了每种算法的最坏情况。
因为此方法相当于就地MSD基数排序，所以在分区完成后，256个存储桶将按顺序排列。下一步是对每个桶的内容进行排序，四次排序就完成了。
对于O(N)分区过程，WolfSort需要交换内存中数组大小的8倍，随后的排序过程需要交换内存中数组大小的1/32。
如果没有足够的内存可用，则wolfort会使用QuadSort，它需要交换内存中阵列大小的1/2。
在理想条件下，分配和释放10MB内存与分配和释放80MB内存在计算上没有差别。从历史上看，软件的内存总是很低的，因此后来开发了排序算法，以便尽可能少地使用额外的内存。如今，大多数现代系统都有几个未使用的、闲置的内存。
在分区过程中，每个桶都变得类似于薛定谔的猫，它可以几乎全部使用，也可以完全不使用，或者介于两者之间。根据概率，我们知道实际使用的恰好是1/8。唯一的开销是跟踪每个桶的大小，并在每次添加时检查桶是否没有溢出。
WolfSort主要是一个概念证明，目前它只正确支持无符号32位整数。
在许多平台上，使用malloc()进行内存分配是一个瓶颈。使用更好的内存分配器或永久交换内存是一种可能的解决方案。
下面的测试是在无线用户线GCC 7.4.0(Ubuntu7.4.0-1ubuntu1~18.04.1)上进行的，源代码使用g++-O3-fpermissive基准进行编译。
下面的基准测试是在WSL GCC版本7.4.0(Ubuntu7.4.0-1ubuntu1~18.04.1)上进行的，源代码是用g++-O3 quigadert.cpp编译的。每个测试都运行了100次，并且只报告了最好的运行。应该注意的是，pdq排序不是一个稳定的排序，这就是它在一般订单数据上速度更快的原因。
下图的X轴是元素数，Y轴是执行时间。
note_en:
This document describes a stable adaptive hybrid radix / merge sort named wolfsort. Itis likely the fastest sort written so far for sorting a mixture of random and ordered data.
  While an adaptive merge sort is very fast at sorting ordered data, its inability to effectivelypartition is its greatest weakness. Radix sort on the other hand is unable to take advantage ofsorted data. Wolfsort tries to avoid the worst case of each algorithm.
  Wolfsort uses  quadsort as its adaptive mergesort since it's faster than Timsort. The radix sort used by wolfsort has two primary functions.
   Wolfsort operates like a typical radix sort by creating 256 buckets and dividing each unsigned32 bit integer by 16777216. Without any optimizations this would multiply the memory overheadby 256 times. Being a hybrid sort wolfsort solves this problem by giving each bucket 1/8th ofthe maximum size.
 If the maximum bucket size is reached the radix sort is aborted and quadsort is ran instead.While this may seem wasteful it means in practice that the worst case of each algorithm isavoided at a relatively small cost.
  Because this approach is the equivalent of an in-place MSD Radix sort the 256 buckets arein order once partitioning completes. The next step is to sort the content of each bucketusinge quadsort and wolfsort will be finished.
  Wolfsort requires 8 times the array size in swap memory for the O(n) partitioning process.The sorting process that follows requires 1/32nd the array size in swap memory.
 If not enough memory is available wolfsort falls back on quadsort which requires 1/2 the arraysize in swap memory.
 In ideal conditions there is no computational difference between allocating and freeing 10 MBof memory and allocating and freeing 80 MB of memory. Historically software was always low onmemory, and subsequently sorting algorithms were developed that bent over backwards in orderto use as little additional memory as possible. Nowadays most modern systems have severalgigabytes of memory that are not used and are just sitting idle.
 During the partitioning process each bucket becomes akin to Schrödinger's cat, it may be usedalmost fully, not at all, or somewhere in between. Based on probability we know exactly 1/8thwill be actually used. The only overhead is to keep track of the size of each bucket, and tocheck that the bucket is not overflowing upon each addition.
  Wolfsort is primarily a proof of concept, currently it only properly supports unsigned 32 bit integers.
 Memory allocation with malloc() is a bottleneck on many platforms. Using a better memory allocatoror permanent swap memory is a possible solution.
  The following benchmark was on WSL gcc version 7.4.0 (Ubuntu 7.4.0-1ubuntu1~18.04.1).The source code was compiled using g++ -O3 -fpermissive bench.c.Each test was ran 100 times and only the best run is reported.
  The following benchmark was on WSL gcc version 7.4.0 (Ubuntu 7.4.0-1ubuntu1~18.04.1).The source code was compiled using g++ -O3 quadsort.cpp. Each test was ran 100 timesand only the best run is reported. It should be noted that pdqsort is not a stablesort which is the reason it's faster on generic order data.
      The X axis of the graph below is the number of elements, the Y axis is the execution time.
title: WolfSort：一种超高速混合基数排序算法
title_en: Wolfsort: An ultra-fast hybrid radix sort algorithm

Hacker95是一个CSS样式表，可以将Hacker News样式化为Windows95/98样式的主题。
根据您使用的浏览器的不同，安装方法会有所不同。首先下载Hacker95并解压缩文件夹中的CSS文件：
在打开的窗口中，打开Chrome文件夹(如果不存在，则创建此文件夹)。
将hacker95.css中的所有CSS代码添加到userContent.css文件(如果该文件不存在，请创建此文件)。
在搜索栏中搜索toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets并将此值设置为TRUE(双击该行将在TRUE和FALSE之间切换)
note_en:
Hacker95 is a CSS stylesheet that styles Hacker News into a Windows 95/98 styled theme.
  Method of installation will differ depending on which browser you are using. First download Hacker95 and unzip the CSS file from the folder:
    In the window that opens up, open the  chrome folder (if none exists, make this folder)
 Add all the CSS code from hacker95.css to the  userContent.css file (if it doesn't exist, create this file)
   In the search bar search for  toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets and set this value to  true (double clicking the row will toggle this between true and false)
title: Hacker95.css-让黑客新闻看起来像Windows 95
title_en: Hacker95.css – Make Hacker News look like Windows 95

note:
周四，一名联邦法官发布了一项为期14天的命令，暂时禁止联邦官员对记者或法律观察员使用武力、威胁和驱散命令，这些命令记录了波特兰市中心每天的示威活动。
在当天早些时候的一次听证会上，美国地区法官迈克尔·H·西蒙(Michael H.Simon)援引美国第九巡回上诉法院的判例法说：“当不当行为正在进行时，官员们有很大的动机蒙住第四遗产的眼睛。新闻自由是公共利益的守护者，司法是新闻的守护者。“。
代表俄勒冈州美国公民自由联盟(American Civil Liberties Union Of Oregon)的律师马修·博登(Matthew Borden)敦促法官“停止联邦特工对波特兰的占领”，并提交了至少12份来自记者、摄影记者和法律观察员的声明，这些人上个月在美国法院外被联邦官员发射的冲击性弹药击中了背部、颈部和腿部。
他说：“这不是意外。这些都不是不经意的射击，“波登说。“这些都是暴君的恐吓行为，它们在波特兰市没有立足之地，在这个国家也没有立足之地。”
西蒙对联邦官员的临时限制令与他本月早些时候授予的管理波特兰警方的临时限制令类似，不同之处在于，一个重大区别是，如果个别联邦官员或主管故意无视他的裁决，他们将被追究责任。
与波特兰警方一样，联邦官员对无意中违反法院命令的行为不承担责任。例如，如果一名记者或法律观察员在警察发出合法命令离开后留在一个地区后，“无意中暴露在人群控制装置中”，那么联邦官员将不会对违规行为负责。
但法官写道，任何“故意违反他的命令，或者联邦主管或指挥官无视或违反该命令的任何指令，都将被视为违反了”明确确立的宪法权利“，因此不会通过限定豁免权的法律原则来保护警官。
有条件的豁免权是美国最高法院50年来的一项原则，旨在保护政府雇员免受轻率的诉讼，但当警察被指控过度使用武力时，往往可以保护警察在民事诉讼中免受责任。
法官下令在24小时内将他长达22页的裁决广泛分发给代理国土安全部部长、美国司法部长威廉·巴尔(William Barr)、部署在波特兰的所有联邦官员，包括正在或可能成为特朗普政府两项倡议-“勤奋勇气行动”或“传奇行动”一部分的任何在波特兰的联邦人员。
美国司法部律师安德鲁·沃登(Andrew Warden)在听证会上辩称，联邦官员很难区分和平示威者和暴力示威者。他代表美国国土安全部和美国法警服务局。
典狱长说，联邦官员必须在瞬间做出决定，并戴着口罩、头盔和其他面罩。他说，当商业烟花向他们投掷，激光照射到他们的眼睛里，在他们之前的每个人都拿出手机拍摄他们的行动时，他们不能停下来确定谁是媒体成员或法律观察员。
他说，联邦官员正面临威胁公共安全的“暴力机会主义者”的特殊情况。他说，新闻媒体“在公众之外没有特别的访问权”。
西蒙写道：“原告被确认为媒体，没有从事非法活动或抗议，没有站在抗议者附近，但受到联邦特工的暴力侵害。”“与联邦被告的论点相反，这一证据并不支持对原告使用武力是人群控制的‘意想不到的后果’。”
法官将矛头对准了这样的论点，即当政府“关闭”公共街道时，记者无权逗留、观察或记录。
“这种循环逻辑对联邦被告没有帮助。首先，联邦被告不是‘关闭’州立公共街道和公园的实体；这是当地警察的职能，“法官指出。“第二，记者观察和记录政府的行动，是为了记录‘封闭’公共街道(例如宣布暴动)是否合法发起和实施。没有记者和法律观察员，只能从政府方面来解释为什么宣布‘暴动’，公共街道被‘封闭’，以及执法部门在执行这一命令时是否采取了适当的行动。“。
由于典狱长在法庭上承认，联邦官员没有证据表明有任何记者或法律观察员在示威期间犯罪，法官裁定，“允许记者和法律观察员留下来、观察和记录事件，不会直接影响到保护联邦财产和联邦官员安全的声明。”
来自波特兰市的律师支持美国公民自由联盟要求临时限制令的要求。
7月2日，西蒙免除了持有专业或授权记者证的记者和来自美国公民自由联盟(ACLU)、全国律师协会(National Lawers Guild)和其他法律团体的观察员必须遵守波特兰警方的命令，要求抗议者在宣布为非法集会或骚乱期间驱散。法官还指示警方不要逮捕、威胁逮捕或对“他们知道或理应知道”的人--记者或法律观察员--进行逮捕、威胁逮捕或使用武力。命令说，警察不能没收他们的相机、音频或录像设备或记者证，除非他们有可能有理由相信已经发生了犯罪。
西蒙后来将针对波特兰警方的临时限制令延长为初步禁令，该市在与原告的联合规定中接受了这一禁令。禁令将于10月30日到期。
该市高级副检察官丹尼斯·万尼尔(Denis Vannier)表示，该市支持美国公民自由联盟(ACLU)限制联邦官员的举措，很大程度上是因为他所说的“联邦辩护律师的法律立场与实际情况之间的脱节”。
他指出，大量记者被联邦官员发射的杀伤力较低的弹药击中，并提醒法院，周三晚上，市长特德·惠勒(Ted Wheeler)在联邦法院前的围栏外遭到催泪瓦斯袭击。
万尼尔认为，联邦官员使用武力与他们面临的威胁不成比例，而且“尽管市政府一再要求”他们留在法院内，最终完全离开波特兰，但这种情况仍在发生。
Vannier表示，在波特兰使用联邦官员似乎是唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统精心策划的决定，目的是将主要是自由、进步的城市作为帮助他在11月当选的政治噱头。
该市副检察官表示，虽然对波特兰警方实施的21天限制有时具有挑战性，但它们是可行的。
法官问联邦政府的律师：“为什么这对市政府和警察局来说是可行的，而对部署在这里的联邦特工来说是不可行的？”
典狱长将联邦法院外的情况描述为“晚上相当混乱的情况”，并表示将人群中的“每个人”隔离将是一项“艰难的努力”。
典狱长说，他并不知道所有联邦部队都接受过专门的训练，但他说，美国海关和边境巡逻队(BORTAC)的战术部队曾接受过应对大型集会的训练。该部队通常被分配到南部边境入境口岸。
西蒙说，他认为媒体继续观察和记录法院外街道上发生的事情至关重要。他指出，最近53岁的海军退伍军人克里斯托弗·大卫(Christopher David)遭到警棍殴打，他试图与联邦特工交谈，而不是威胁任何人。
法官说，大卫被一名联邦官员袭击，尽管他“没有可能的犯罪原因”。
西蒙还谈到了26岁的多纳万·拉贝拉(Donavan LaBella)，他在7月中旬被一名美国副法警击中头部，当时他的双手举着法院对面的一个音乐扬声器。
他还提到了两名联邦官员开着没有标志的面包车从街上接人的描述。
西蒙指出，虽然拉贝拉和大卫不是记者或法律观察员，但记者报道了他们身上发生的事情。
法官指出了摄影记者内森·霍华德(Nathan Howard)的宣誓声明，霍华德说，周日晚些时候到周一凌晨，他正在西南第三大道附近的查普曼广场(Chapman Square)，一群联邦官员走出法院，将抗议者推回第四大道。
霍华德说，他留在广场上是因为他想记录另一群联邦官员，他们来自伊迪丝·格林-温德尔·怀亚特联邦大楼(Edith Green-Wendell Wyatt Federal Building)向南两个街区。他说，除了其他记者，他周围几乎没有人。
当一队联邦官员向前走时，霍华德说他尖叫着，“我是新闻界，我是新闻界！”一只手举着他的记者证，另一只手举着相机。小组中的特工告诉他呆在原地不动，然后继续前进。
后来，当两组联邦特工合并并开始在广场之间行走时，霍华德说他尖叫着说他是“记者！”再来一次。一名警官告诉他“呆在原地不动，不要靠近”，但三秒钟后，另一名联邦警官直接向他发射了至少两个胡椒球。
“他们完全没有合理的理由相信那个人犯了罪，”西蒙说。“这就是我认为原告试图在此止步的原因.”

note_en:
A federal judge on Thursday issued a 14-day order temporarily barring federal officers from using force, threats and dispersal orders against journalists or legal observers documenting the daily demonstrations in downtown Portland.
 During a hearing earlier in the day, U.S. District Judge Michael H. Simon quoted case law from the 9th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals, saying, “When wrongdoing is underway, officials have great incentive to blindfold the eyes of the Fourth Estate. The free press is the guardian of the public interest, and the judiciary is the guardian of the press.”
  Attorney Matthew Borden, representing the American Civil Liberties Union of Oregon, urged the judge to “stop the federal agents’ occupation of Portland” and submitted at least a dozen statements from journalists, photojournalists and legal observers who have suffered shots to the back, neck and legs from impact munitions fired by federal officers outside the Mark O. Hatfield U.S. Courthouse in the last month.
  “These are not accidents. These are not inadvertent shots,” Borden said. “These are acts of intimidation by a tyrant and they have no place in the city of Portland and they have no place in this country.‘'
  Simon’s temporary restraining order for federal officers is similar to one he granted earlier this month governing Portland police, except for one major difference that allows for individual federal officers or supervisors to be held liable if they intentionally disregard his ruling.
  As with Portland police, federal officers will not be liable for unintentional violations of the court order. For example, if a journalist or legal observer is “incidentally exposed to crowd-control devices” after remaining in an area after officers give a lawful order to leave, then federal officers won’t be liable for a violation.
  But the judge wrote that any “willful violation'' of his order or any direction by a federal supervisor or commander to disregard or violate the order will be considered a violation of “a clearly established constitutional right” and therefore won’t shield the officer through the legal doctrine of qualified immunity.
  Qualified immunity is a 50-year-old principle from the U.S. Supreme Court meant to protect government employees from frivolous litigation but often shields police from accountability in civil suits when officers are accused of using excessive force.
  The judge ordered that his  22-page ruling be widely distributed within 24 hours to U.S. Attorney General William Barr, the acting Homeland Security secretary, all federal officers deployed in Portland, including any federal personnel in Portland who are or may become part of “Operation Diligent Valor” or “Operation Legend” -- two Trump administration initiatives.
  Attorney Andrew Warden of the U. S Department of Justice argued at the hearing that it would be too difficult for federal officers to distinguish peaceful demonstrators from violent ones. He represents the U.S. Department of Homeland Security and U.S. Marshals Service.
  Warden said the federal officers must make split-second decisions and are wearing masks, helmets and other face-coverings. They can’t stop to determine who’s a member of the press or a legal observer when commercial fireworks are being thrown at them and lasers shined in their eyes and everyone before them has their cellphone out filming their actions, he said.
  Federal officers, he said, are facing extraordinary circumstances from “violent opportunists” who are threatening public safety. The press is guaranteed “no special rights of access above and beyond the public,” he said.
    “The plaintiffs were identifiable as press, not engaging in unlawful activity or protesting, were not standing near protesters, and yet were subject to violence by federal agents,” Simon wrote. “Contrary to the Federal Defendants’ arguments, this evidence does not support that the force used on Plaintiffs were ‘unintended consequences’ of crowd control.”
  The judge zeroed in on the argument that journalists don’t have a right to stay, observe or document when the government “closes” public streets.
  “This circular logic does not help the Federal Defendants. First, the Federal Defendants are not the entities that ‘close’ state public streets and parks; that is a local police function,” the judge noted. “Second, the point of journalists observing and documenting government action is to record whether the ‘closing’ of public streets (e.g. , declaring a riot) is lawfully originated and carried out. Without journalists and legal observers, there is only the government’s side of the story to explain why a ‘riot’ was declared and the public streets were ‘closed’ and whether law enforcement acted properly in effectuating that order.”
  As Warden acknowledged in court that the federal officers had no evidence of any journalists or legal observers committing crimes during the demonstrations, the judge ruled, “The stated need to protect federal property and the safety of federal officers is not directly affected by allowing journalists and legal observers to stay, observe, and record events.”
  Attorneys from the city of Portland supported the ACLU’s request for a temporary restraining order.
  On July 2, Simon exempted journalists with professional or authorized press passes and observers from the ACLU, National Lawyers Guild and other legal groups from having to follow Portland police orders requiring protesters to disperse during declared unlawful assemblies or riots. The judge also directed police not to arrest, threaten to arrest or use force against a person “who they know or reasonably should know” is a journalist or legal observer. Officers aren’t to seize their cameras, audio or videotaping equipment or press passes unless they have probable cause to believe a crime has been committed, the order says.
  Simon later extended the temporary restraining order against Portland police to a preliminary injunction that the city accepted in a joint stipulation with the plaintiffs. The injunction expires Oct. 30.
  Denis Vannier, senior deputy city attorney, said the city backed the ACLU’s move to restrict federal officers largely because of what he called “the disconnect between the federal defense’s legal position and really the facts on the ground.”
  He noted that large numbers of journalists have been struck by less-lethal munitions fired by federal officers and reminded the court that Mayor Ted Wheeler was tear-gassed among protesters outside the fence in front of the federal courthouse Wednesday night.
  Vannier argued the use of force by federal officers is disproportionate to the threat they face and is “happening despite the city’s repeated request” for them to stay inside the courthouse and ultimately leave Portland altogether.
  Vannier said the use of the federal officers in Portland appears to have been a calculated decision by President Donald Trump to target largely liberal, progressive cities in a political stunt to help his election in November.
  The deputy city attorney said while the restrictions placed on Portland police, in effect for 21 days, have been challenging at times, they’ve been workable.
  The judge asked the lawyer for the federal government: “Why would it be workable for the city and the Police Bureau and not workable for the federal agents who have been deployed here?”
  Warden described the circumstances outside the federal courthouse as a " fairly extraordinarily chaotic situation” at night and that “segregating everyone” in the crowd would be a “difficult endeavor.”
    Warden said he wasn’t aware of all the federal units’ specialized training but said the U.S. Customs and Border Patrol’s tactical unit, or BORTAC, has training in dealing with large gatherings. The unit is typically assigned to the southern border ports of entry.
  Simon said he believes it’s critically important for the press to continue to observe and document what’s happening on the street outside the courthouse. He noted the recent baton-battering of 53-year-old Christopher David, a Navy veteran trying to speak with federal agents and not threatening anyone.
  David was assaulted by a federal officer despite the fact “there was no probable cause he was committing a crime,” the judge said.
  Simon also spoke of 26-year-old Donavan LaBella, who in mid-July was shot in the head by a deputy U.S. marshal while he had his hands above his head holding a music speaker across from the courthouse.
  He also referenced two accounts of federal officers in unmarked vans picking people up off the street.
  Simon noted that while LaBella and David aren’t journalists or legal observers, accounts of what happened to them were reported by journalists.
  The judge pointed to the sworn statement from photojournalist Nathan Howard, who said he was in Chapman Square near Southwest Third Avenue late Sunday into early Monday when a group of federal officers emerged from the courthouse and pushed protesters back to Fourth Avenue.
  Howard said he remained in the square because he wanted to document another group of federal officers coming from the Edith Green-Wendell Wyatt Federal Building two blocks south. There were few people around him except other journalists, he said.
  As a line of federal officers advanced, Howard said he screamed, “I’m press, I’m press!” and held up his press pass in one hand and camera in the other. Agents in the group told him to stay put and moved on.
  Later, as two groups of federal agents merged and began walking between the square, Howard said he screamed that he was “press!” again. One officer told him to “stay where you are, don’t come close,” but three seconds later, another federal officer fired at least two pepper balls directly at him.
  “And they had absolutely no probable cause to believe that person committed a crime,‘' Simon said. “That’s what I think the plaintiffs are trying to stop here.”

预告片和预告片不断从虚拟的圣地亚哥动漫展@Home传来，整个周末都在上映。HBO取消了他的“黑暗材料”第二季的第一个预告片，这是根据菲利普·普尔曼的畅销奇幻三部曲改编的。虽然第二季本应该有八集，就像第一季一样，但由于大流行，制作被停产了。所以只会有七集。
该系列的三本书于1995年首次出版，分别是《黄金指南针》(在英国以北极光出版)、《微妙的刀》和《琥珀望远镜》。他们讲述了一个名叫莱拉的12岁女孩的冒险故事，她生活在大约维多利亚时代的英国牛津的虚构版本中。每个人都有一个动物形式的守护神陪伴，这是他们灵魂的一部分，驻留在身体之外；天琴座的守护神被命名为潘塔莱蒙(Pantalaimon)。莱拉发现了一个险恶的阴谋，这让她踏上了寻找父亲的旅程，希望能挫败这一阴谋。这段旅程把她带到了不同的维度(虚构的世界是一个多元宇宙)，并最终走向了她自己的成熟。
HBO/BBC One的改编版主要报道了黄金罗盘的事件，尽管S1也包括了微妙的刀中的人物和事件-最值得注意的是，介绍了威尔·帕里(阿米尔·威尔逊饰)这个角色，他和一个在逃的天琴座成了朋友，并拥有这把名义上的刀，可以在宇宙之间拉开帷幕。根据HBO的官方S1概要：
[T]第一季讲述了莱拉(达芙妮·基恩，洛根饰)的故事，她是一位来自另一个世界的看似平凡但勇敢的年轻女子。她寻找一位被绑架的朋友，揭露了一个涉及被盗儿童的邪恶阴谋，并成为一种理解一种名为灰尘的神秘现象的探索。当她穿越世界，包括我们自己的世界时，莱拉遇到了威尔(阿米尔·威尔逊饰)，一个坚定而勇敢的男孩。在一起，他们遇到了非同寻常的生物和危险的秘密，活着的 - 和死去的 - 的命运都掌握在他们的手中。
第一季的评价褒贬不一，但在我们的2019年年终电视综述中，它值得一提。我称它为一部视觉上华丽、节奏巧妙、情节安排得当的电视剧，将普尔曼虚构的多元宇宙生动地展现了出来。达芙妮·基恩完美地饰演了活泼而冒险的主人公莱拉，而露丝·威尔逊紧张的表演展示了库尔特夫人近乎野性的冷酷，以及她狡猾的老练和一丝脆弱。这部电影并不完美，但与2007年参差不齐的改编电影“黄金罗盘”相比，它无疑是一个进步。
在S1大结局中，莱拉终于找到了她的父亲阿斯利尔勋爵(詹姆斯·麦卡沃伊饰)，但她得知他对她最好的朋友罗杰怀有邪恶的图谋，而罗杰是莱拉从博尔万格实验设施中救出的，阿斯利尔勋爵需要一种能量来源来打开多元宇宙世界之间的桥梁，从而进一步推进他的研究。而切断孩子与他们的守护程序是最好的能量来源-除了罗杰在这个过程中被杀。莱拉决定跟随阿斯瑞尔勋爵穿过多元宇宙之桥。与此同时，威尔·帕里发现自己进入了多元宇宙。这就是我们停下来的地方。
阿斯瑞尔勋爵打开了一座通往新世界的桥梁，莱拉(达芙妮·基恩饰)为她最好的朋友的去世而心烦意乱，跟随阿斯瑞尔进入了未知的世界。在一个奇怪而神秘的废弃城市里，她遇到了威尔，一个来自我们这个世界的男孩，他也在逃离麻烦的过去。莱拉和将了解到他们的命运是与团聚的意愿与他的父亲，但发现他们的道路不断受挫，因为一场战争开始酝酿围绕他们。与此同时，库尔特太太寻找莱拉，决心不惜一切必要手段把她带回家。
S2的预告片以莱拉探索新世界开始，在这个世界里，她发现自己在与威尔会面之前就发现了自己。他们都意识到这两个人都不是来自这个世界。与此同时，库尔特夫人的任务是找到莱拉并把她带回家，这是教主卡洛·博莱尔(阿里扬·巴卡雷饰)的任务(他已经找到了自己在世界之间的门户)。她说，外面有多个世界，人们会寻找她--并不是所有的人都是好的。
至于教廷，预告片承诺它决心取得控制权。这将使它与女巫和其他派别发生直接冲突。那是琥珀望远镜里物理学家玛丽·马龙的一瞥吗？
他的“黑暗材料”第二季将于今年秋天在HBO播出。与此同时，你可以在HBO和HBO MAX上赶上S1。
note_en:
The trailers and sneak peeks just keep coming from the virtual San Diego Comic Con@Home, running all this weekend. HBO dropped the first trailer for the second season of   His Dark Materials, an adaptation of the best-selling fantasy trilogy by Philip Pullman. While there were supposed to be eight episodes in this second season, just like S1, production was shut down due to the pandemic. So there will only be seven episodes.
  First published in 1995, the  three books in the series are  The Golden Compass (published as  Northern Lights in the UK),  The Subtle Knife, and  The Amber Spyglass. They follow the adventures of a 12-year-old girl named Lyra, who lives in a fictional version of Oxford, England, circa the Victorian era. Everyone has a companion daemon in the form of an animal—part of their spirit that resides outside the body; Lyra's is named Pantalaimon. Lyra uncovers a sinister plot that sends her on a journey to find her father in hopes of foiling said plot. That journey takes her to different dimensions (the fictional world is a multiverse) and ultimately to her own coming-of-age.
 The HBO/BBC One adaptation mostly covered the events of  The Golden Compass, although S1 also included characters and events from  The Subtle Knife—most notably, introducing the character of Will Parry (Amir Wilson), who befriends an on-the-run Lyra and has possession of the titular knife, which can cut the curtain between universes. Per HBO's official S1 synopsis:
 [T]he first season follows Lyra (Dafne Keen,  Logan), a seemingly ordinary but brave young woman from another world. Her search for a kidnapped friend uncovers a sinister plot involving stolen children, and becomes a quest to understand a mysterious phenomenon called Dust. As she journeys through the worlds, including our own, Lyra meets Will (Amir Wilson), a determined and courageous boy. Together, they encounter extraordinary beings and dangerous secrets, with the fate of both the living — and the dead — in their hands.
 The first season met with mixed reviews, but it warranted an honorable mention in our 2019 year-end TV roundup.  I called it "a visually gorgeous, deftly paced and plotted series that brings Pullman's fictional multiverse to vivid life. Dafne Keen is perfectly cast as the feisty and adventurous main protagonist, Lyra, while Ruth Wilson's taut performance brings out Mrs. Coulter's almost feral ruthlessness, along with her cunning sophistication and a hint of vulnerability. It's not perfect, but it's definitely an improvement over the uneven 2007 film adaptation,  The Golden Compass."
    In the S1 finale, Lyra finally finds her father, Lord Asriel (James McAvoy), but she learns that he has nefarious designs on her best friend Roger, whom Lyra had rescued from the Bolvangar experimental facility. Lord Asriel needs a source of energy to open a bridge between the worlds of the multiverse, thereby furthering his research. And severing a child from their daemon is the best source of that energy—except Roger is killed in the process. Lyra decides to follow Lord Asriel across the multiverse bridge. Meanwhile, Will Parry discovers his own entrance into the multiverse. That's where we left off.
  Lord Asriel has opened a bridge to a new world, and, distraught over the death of her best friend, Lyra (Dafne Keen) follows Asriel into the unknown. In a strange and mysterious abandoned city, she meets Will, a boy from our world who is also running from a troubled past. Lyra and Will learn their destinies are tied to reuniting Will with his father but find their path is constantly thwarted as a war begins to brew around them. Meanwhile, Mrs. Coulter searches for Lyra, determined to bring her home by any means necessary.
 The trailer for S2 opens with Lyra exploring the new world in which she finds herself before meeting up with Will. They both realize neither is from this world. Meanwhile, Mrs. Coulter has tasked Lord Carlo Boreal (Ariyon Bakare) of the Magisterium (who has found his own gateway between worlds) with finding Lyra and bringing her home. "There are multiple worlds out there, and people will be looking for her—not all of them good," she says.
 As for the Magisterium, the trailer promises that it is determined to "take control." That will put it in direct conflict with a coven of witches, among other factions. And is that a glimpse of physicist Mary Malone from  The Amber Spyglass?
 The second season of  His Dark Materials will air this fall on HBO. In the meantime you can catch up on S1 on HBO and HBO Max.
title: HBO在圣地亚哥Comic-Con@Home首次亮相他的黑暗材料S2预告片
title_en: HBO debuts first His Dark Materials S2 trailer at San Diego Comic-Con@Home

据彭博社(Bloomberg)最先报道的一起新诉讼显示，特斯拉表示，它发现了一种“令人不安的模式”，即前员工在前往电动汽车初创公司Rivian担任新工作的途中窃取机密信息和商业机密。特斯拉甚至声称里维安“故意鼓励”这种行为，它正在为其所称的“卑鄙、肆意、压迫、故意、恶意和欺骗”的行为寻求未指明的惩罚性赔偿。里维安称这些指控“毫无根据”。
Rivian可能是世界上最富有的电动汽车初创公司，在过去一年半的时间里筹集了超过50亿美元。该公司计划在2021年初开始交付一款优质电动皮卡，提前于特斯拉的CyberTruck上路。该公司还将同时开始销售一款电动SUV。诉讼称，Rivian在此次发布之前已经从汽车和科技行业的一流公司招聘了约2400名员工，其中包括178名前特斯拉员工，其中70人直接跳槽。
这起诉讼是在上周晚些时候提起的，将四名前特斯拉员工和里维安列为被告，尽管特斯拉表示，它已经确认了另外一些人，他们可能也窃取了公司的机密信息，并将机密信息带给了这家电动汽车初创公司。
里维安的一位发言人在一份声明中说：“我们钦佩特斯拉在重新设定人们对电动汽车的期望方面发挥的领导作用。”他说：“加入里维安公司后，我们要求所有员工确认他们没有，也不会将前雇主的知识产权引入里维安公司的系统。这起诉讼的指控毫无根据，与里维安的文化、精神和公司政策背道而驰。“。
特斯拉表示，其中两名被点名的被告承认获取了机密信息。其中之一是特斯拉人事部门的高级经理塔米·帕斯卡尔(Tami Pascale)。特斯拉表示，在帕斯卡尔签署里维安的聘书一天后，她“从特斯拉的关系网中拿到了至少10份机密和专有文件”，包括候选人名单、这家汽车制造商在哪里找到潜在员工的信息，以及一份“高管级别候选人的详细内部报告”。
特斯拉表示，帕斯卡尔在7月初被公司调查团队质问时最初否认了这一点，但她最终“承认拿走了机密和专有文件”。然而，据称帕斯卡尔并不同意删除这些文件，该公司声称她仍然保留着她的工作笔记本电脑。特斯拉说，她与该公司的一名调查人员共享了手机屏幕，当她被要求搜索公司名称时，可以看到“大量文件”，但帕斯卡尔“突然结束了会话”。
据称，特斯拉环境、健康和安全部门的经理杰西卡·西伦在里维安的邀请函上签字三天后，向她的个人Gmail账户发送了文件。特斯拉声称，Sron最初在面对调查小组时否认这样做，但她承认在被追问时发送了一份文件。
除了直到几周前还是特斯拉员工招聘人员的金·黄(Kim Wong)的案件外，特斯拉的投诉没有透露里维安知晓或鼓励任何不当行为的细节。起诉书称，特斯拉的一名招聘经理联系了王，这名经理告诉她，“里维安没有增加初创公司招聘工作所需的招聘模板、结构、公式或文件”。特斯拉表示，就在那次对话的同一天，王菲从特斯拉的网络向她的Gmail账户发送了“至少16份高度机密的招聘文件”，其中包括机密的Powerpoint演示文稿，其中包含了特斯拉招聘和招聘流程的细节，以及工资信息。
据特斯拉称，里维安的副总法律顾问对这些指控采取了“漫不经心的态度”，并“声称窃取机密信息在该行业很常见。”里维安告诉The Verge，它不同意这种框定。“本着诚意，我们与特斯拉讨论了我们对待任何指控的严肃性。这份文件歪曲了律师之间的对话，“这位发言人说。
特斯拉在诉讼中表示，之所以能够弄清楚这一切，是因为其调查团队“最近获得了先进的电子安全监控工具”。多年来，特斯拉的调查团队不止一次被指控对员工进行黑客和间谍活动。
特斯拉的诉讼只是硅谷运输公司之间源源不断的商业秘密之争中的最新一起。最值得注意的是Waymo对优步(Uber)的诉讼，该公司指控安东尼·莱万多夫斯基(Anthony Levandowski)窃取了大量自动驾驶汽车的数据，并与时任首席执行官特拉维斯·卡兰尼克(Travis Kalanick)合谋将这些数据提供给优步。(诉讼最终达成和解。)。
这也不是特斯拉的第一起商业秘密诉讼。该公司最近就一起针对自动驾驶初创公司Zoox的诉讼达成和解，导致Zoox承认其从特斯拉雇佣的多名员工携带被盗的文件抵达。2019年，特斯拉起诉一名前员工涉嫌向中国电动汽车初创公司XPeng窃取商业机密。这起诉讼正在进入发现阶段，但这名前员工在去年的一份法庭文件中已经承认，他将Autopilot源代码上传到了他的个人iCloud账户。
2018年，一名前苹果员工因涉嫌窃取商业机密而被捕，当时他也试图在XPeng找到一份工作。
note_en:
Tesla says it has discovered a “disturbing pattern” of former employees who took confidential information and trade secrets on their way to new jobs at EV startup Rivian, according to  a new lawsuit first reported by   Bloomberg. Tesla even claims Rivian is “knowingly encouraging” this behavior, and it is seeking unspecified punitive damages for what it alleges is “despicable, wanton, oppressive, willful, malicious, [and] duplicitous” conduct. Rivian calls the allegations “baseless.”
 Rivian is perhaps the richest EV startup in the world, having raised  more than $5 billion in the last year and a half. It’s slated to start delivering a premium electric pickup truck in early 2021, ahead of when Tesla’s Cybertruck is supposed to hit the road. The company will also start selling an electric SUV at the same time. Rivian has hired some 2,400 employees ahead of this launch from  top-flight companies across the automotive and tech industries, including 178 former Tesla workers, according to the lawsuit, with 70 making the jump directly.
  The lawsuit, filed late last week, names four former Tesla employees and Rivian as defendants, though Tesla says it has identified additional people who may have also stolen and brought confidential company information to the EV startup.
 “We admire Tesla for its leadership in resetting expectations of what an electric car can be,” a spokesperson for Rivian said in a statement. “Upon joining Rivian, we require all employees to confirm that they have not, and will not, introduce former employers’ intellectual property into Rivian systems. This suit’s allegations are baseless and run counter to Rivian’s culture, ethos and corporate policies.”
 Tesla says two of the named defendants admitted to taking confidential information. One is Tami Pascale, who was a senior manager in Tesla’s staffing department. Tesla says that one day after Pascale signed Rivian’s offer letter, she “took at least ten confidential and proprietary documents from Tesla’s network,” including candidate lists, information about where the automaker finds potential hires, and a “detailed internal write-up of an executive level candidate.”
  Tesla says Pascale initially denied this when confronted by the company’s investigative team in early July, but that she ultimately “confessed to taking the confidential and proprietary documents.” Pascale allegedly did not agree to delete the files, though, and the company claims she still has her work laptop. Tesla says she shared the screen of her phone with one of the company’s investigators, and that when she was asked to search for the company’s name, “numerous files” were visible, but Pascale “abruptly ended the session.”
 Jessica Siron, who was a manager in Tesla’s environmental, health, and safety department, allegedly sent documents to her personal Gmail account three days after signing an offer letter from Rivian. Tesla claims Siron initially denied doing this when confronted by its investigative team, but that she admitted to sending one document when pressed.
 Tesla’s complaint is light on details about Rivian’s knowledge or encouragement of any wrongdoing, save for the case of Kim Wong, who was a staff recruiter at Tesla up until just a few weeks ago. Tesla claims Wong was contacted by a Rivian hiring manager who told her “Rivian did not have the recruiting templates, structures, formulas, or documents that would be needed” to grow the startup’s recruiting efforts, according to the complaint. The same day as that conversation, Tesla says Wong sent “at least sixteen highly confidential recruiting documents from Tesla’s network to her Gmail account,” including confidential Powerpoint presentations that contained details about the automaker’s recruiting and hiring process, as well as salary information.
  Rivian’s associate general counsel, according to Tesla, took a “cavalier attitude” toward the accusations and “claimed that taking confidential information was common in the industry.” Rivian tells  The Verge it disagrees with this framing. “In good faith, we discussed with Tesla the seriousness with which we take any allegation. This document misrepresents a conversation between counsel,” the spokesperson said.
 Tesla says in the lawsuit that it was able to figure all this out because its investigative team “recently acquired sophisticated electronic security monitoring tools.” Tesla’s investigative team has, over the years, been  accused of hacking and spying on employees  more than once.
 Tesla’s lawsuit is just the latest in what has become a constant stream of trade secret fights among Silicon Valley transportation companies. Most notable was Waymo’s lawsuit against Uber, wherein it accused Anthony Levandowski of stealing troves of data about self-driving cars and conspiring with then-CEO Travis Kalanick to shepherd that data to Uber. (The suit  was ultimately settled.)
 This is not Tesla’s first trade secret suit, either. It recently settled  a lawsuit against self-driving startup Zoox, which resulted in Zoox admitting that multiple employees it hired from Tesla arrived with stolen documents. In 2019, Tesla sued a former employee for  allegedly taking trade secrets to Chinese EV startup XPeng. That lawsuit is entering the discovery phase, but the former employee already admitted in a court filing last year that  he uploaded Autopilot source code to his personal iCloud account.
 A former Apple employee was arrested in 2018 for allegedly stealing trade secrets  as he tried to get a job with XPeng, too.
title: 特斯拉指控里维安挖走员工，窃取机密

note:
在这一点上，每个人都应该清楚地看到，科技行业存在多样性问题。很多人都写过这方面的文章，但我最喜欢的一篇关于这件事的博客帖子是由Dan Luu写的。在视频中，他链接到Twitter上的一个帖子，罗杰·迪基(Roger Dickey)在那里表达了他的“不受欢迎的观点”，即招聘女性/少数族裔很难，因为存在管道问题。基本上，罗杰认为没有足够的女性或有色人种从STEM项目毕业，所以他们中没有足够的人创办公司来资助或进入应聘者池中招聘。以下是我最喜欢的回答：
在该行业雇佣、推荐和邀请…参加派对的人方面，存在着普遍的偏见。
我曾经在一家公司工作(我们就叫它Hooli吧)，那里的招聘人员每年秋天都会去不同的大学巡回演出，希望能招聘到一些闪亮的应届大学毕业生。在某种程度上，他们要去我的家乡北卡罗来纳州的大学做一些平常的事情：做一些演讲，让学生们对胡利的使命感到兴奋，关于#Impact之类的东西。行程中的学院之一是北卡罗来纳农工大学，这是一所历史悠久的黑人学院/大学，也是一所非常好的学院。由于我是Hooli办公室仅有的3名黑人工程师之一，而且是卡罗莱纳男孩，招聘团队坚持让我陪他们走这段旅程。去北卡罗来纳州的免费旅行？算上我一个。
第一次招聘活动是为杜克大学/北卡罗来纳大学的学生举办的，包括一小时的演示，内容包括在Hooli的生活，现有的各种团队，面试过程是什么样子，如何为面试做准备，然后是一张包含招聘人员联系信息的幻灯片。然后是一个半小时的分组会议，学生们组成一个小(约5人)小组，然后有机会在餐桌上向Hooli工程师询问他们在餐饮晚餐时想要的任何东西。这也是他们给人留下好印象的一种方式，在我们收集他们所有的简历，标记出哪些看起来最令人印象深刻，并邀请其中一半人第二天晚上在一家高档餐厅共进晚餐之前，这也是一种方式。哇，说到铺开红地毯！
第二场招聘活动是第二天下午在NC A&amp；T举行的。招聘人员把前一天晚上剩下的餐饮剩饭拿出来，匆匆翻阅了前一天晚上的简短陈述(30分钟)。没有分组表格，没有简历收集，没有招聘者联系方式...。我个人订了一堆披萨，因为没有人认为这是个问题。我气坏了。披萨送到后，招聘者分发了一些Hooli礼品，鼓励每个人申请，然后打包行李，这样每个人都可以开车2个小时回到达勒姆，与另一组学生共进第二顿晚餐。
第一组学生的人口统计数据反映了你在典型的硅谷科技公司中会看到的大致情况。这些人在这次旅行中受到了所有的关注和所有的好处。
杜克大学/北卡罗来纳大学的活动是在晚上举行的，所以没有人需要缺课来参加。许多A&A；T的学生不能参加整个活动，因为当时是中午，他们要上课。
杜克大学/北卡罗来纳大学的学生们有多次机会以小组形式与我们交谈。通过延长的演示，他们收到了更多关于面试过程、学习什么以及Hooli的信息。他们也有机会给我们留下深刻印象，并详细介绍了他们的背景和经验。A&amp；T的学生们几乎没有机会做自我介绍，胡利就收拾好行李，去和另一组人共进第二顿晚餐。
A&A；T的学生们吃到了前一天晚上剩下的食物。操。你认为这传递了什么样的信息？如果一家公司的招聘人员是这样对待你的，你会觉得申请工作或实习会受到鼓励吗？
我敢肯定，NC A&amp；T的活动只是为了让Hooli可以勾选一些“多元化招聘”的复选框。很明显，他们实际上并没有认真对待那些学生。通过对北卡罗来纳大学/杜克大学的学生表现出更多的关注，并在NC A&T；的活动中过半，这些A&A；T的学生处于不利地位。为什么？Hooli招聘人员认为他们不会通过招聘门槛吗？
这就是女人和有色人种正在经历的那种胡言乱语。难怪当甲板像这样堆放在对他们不利的地方时，就会缺乏多样性。管道问题，混蛋。
note_en:
It should be obvious to everyone at this point that the tech industry has a diversity problem. Lots of folks have written about this, but one of my favorite blog post on the matter is  written by Dan Luu. In it, he links to a  Twitter thread where Roger Dickey expresses his “unpopular opinion” that hiring women/minorities is hard because there is a  pipeline problem. Basically, Roger thinks that there are not enough women or people of color graduating from STEM programs, so there aren’t enough of them starting companies to fund or entering the candidate pool to hire. Here’s my favorite response:
  There’s a pervasive bias in who the industry hires, refers, and invites to parties…
  I used to work for a company (let’s call it Hooli), where recruiters would go on tour to various universities each fall with the hopes of recruiting some shiny new college grads. At some point they were going to colleges in my home state of North Carolina to do the usual: give some presentations, get the students excited about Hooli’s mission, something about #impact and whatnot. One of the colleges on the itinerary was North Carolina A&T,  a historically black college/university, and a  damn good one. Since I was one of only  3 black engineers in the Hooli office and a Carolina Boy, the recruiting team insisted that I accompany them on this leg of the tour. Free trip to NC? Count me in.
 The first recruiting event was for Duke/UNC students and consisted of a 1-hour presentation which covered life at Hooli, various teams that exist, what the interview process is like, how to prepare for the interview, and then a slide containing the recruiter’s contact info. This was then followed by a 1.5 hour break-out session where the students formed small (~5 person) groups and then had a chance to ask a Hooli engineer at their table anything they want over a   catered dinner . This was also a way for them to make a good impression before we collected all of their resumes, marked which ones seemed the most impressive, and  invited half of them to dinner at a fancy restaurant the next evening. Wow, talk about rolling out the red carpet!
 The second recruiting event was the following afternoon at NC A&T. Recruiters  put out the catering leftovers from the previous night and rushed through a shortened (30 minute) version of the previous night’s presentation. There were no break-out tables, no resume collection, no recruiter contact details... I personally ordered a bunch of pizzas because nobody thought that this was a problem. I was  livid. After the pizzas arrived, recruiters handed out some Hooli swag, encouraged everyone to apply, and packed up so that everyone could make the 2-hour drive back to Durham in time for a  second dinner with the other group of students.
  The demographics of the first group of students reflected  pretty much what you’d expect to find in your typical Silicon Valley tech company. Those were the people that received all of the attention and all of the advantages on this trip.
 The Duke/UNC events were in the evening, so nobody had to miss class to attend. Many of the A&T students couldn’t attend the entire event because it was in the  middle of the day and they had class.
 The Duke/UNC students had multiple chances to talk with us in small groups. They received more information about the interview process, what to study, and Hooli via the extended presentation. They also had chances to impress us and elaborate on their backgrounds and experience. The A&T students barely got a chance to introduce themselves before Hooli packed up and left for  a second dinner with the other group.
 The A&T students were given  leftover food from the night before. Fuck. What kind of message do you think this sends? Would you feel encouraged to apply for a job or internship if that’s how a company’s recruiters treated you?
 I’m sure that the NC A&T event was just so Hooli could check some “diversity recruitment” box. It’s apparent that they didn’t actually take those students seriously. By showing more attention to the UNC/Duke students and half-assing the event at NC A&T, those A&T students were put at a disadvantage. Why? Did Hooli recruiters think that they wouldn’t pass the hiring bar?
 This is the kind of nonsense women and people of color are going through. No wonder there’s a lack of diversity when the deck is stacked against them like this. Pipeline problem, my ass.

note:
2019年5月，KrebsOnSecurity爆出消息称，抵押贷款所有权保险巨头第一美国金融公司(First American Financial Corp.)的网站曝光了大约8.85亿条与2003年抵押贷款交易有关的记录。周三，纽约的监管机构宣布，First American是他们有史以来第一次与这起事件有关的网络安全执法行动的目标，这些指控可能会带来巨额的经济处罚。
总部位于加利福尼亚州圣安娜的First American[纽约证券交易所代码：FAF]是房地产和抵押贷款行业产权保险和结算服务的领先提供商。它雇佣了大约1.8万名员工，2019年的收入为62亿美元。
正如去年在这里首次报道的那样，First American的网站披露了16年的数字化抵押贷款所有权保险记录-包括银行账号和对账单、抵押贷款和税务记录、社会保障号码、电汇交易收据和驾照图像。
根据纽约州金融服务部(DFS)提交的一份文件(PDF)，暴露这些文件的漏洞最初是在2014年5月的一次应用软件更新期间引入的，多年来一直没有被发现。
更糟糕的是，DFS发现，该漏洞是在2018年12月First American自行进行的渗透测试中发现的。
DFS在一份关于指控的声明中解释说：“值得注意的是，响应方反而允许数百万客户在六个月内不受限制地获取个人和财务数据，直到这起入侵事件及其严重后果被一名全国公认的网络安全行业记者广泛报道。”
路透社(Reuters)报道称，对First American的处罚可能会很严重：DFS将每一次泄露个人信息的情况视为单独的违规行为，该公司每次违规将面临最高1000美元的罚款。
在一份书面声明中，First American表示，它强烈反对DFS的调查结果，它自己的调查确定，只有“非常有限的数量”的消费者--没有来自纽约的消费者--在未经许可的情况下访问了个人数据。
2019年8月，该公司表示，对曝光的第三方调查发现，只有32名消费者的非公开个人信息可能在未经授权的情况下被访问。
去年，当KrebsOnSecurity询问它保留了多长时间的访问日志，或者审查进行了多长时间后，First American拒绝透露更多细节，只是说它的日志涵盖了一段时间，这对于像它这样规模和性质的公司来说是典型的。
但在周三的文件中，DFS表示，第一美国人无法确定记录是否在2018年6月之前被访问。
DFS发现：“被告的法医调查依赖于对2018年6月以来保留的网络日志的审查。”“被访者自己的分析显示，在这11个月期间，超过35万份文件被自动‘机器人’或‘刮刀’程序未经授权访问，这些程序旨在收集互联网上的信息。
第一美国人曝光的记录将是参与所谓的商业电子邮件妥协(BEC)骗局的钓鱼者和诈骗者的虚拟金矿，这些骗局经常冒充房地产经纪人、关闭机构、所有权和托管公司，以欺骗购房者将资金电汇给欺诈者。根据联邦调查局的说法，BEC诈骗是当今代价最高的网络犯罪形式。
在他们数据泄露的消息在这里发布后的第二天，第一美国公司的股价下跌了6%以上。在接下来的几天里，DFS和美国证券交易委员会(U.S.Securities and Exchange Commission)分别宣布正在调查该公司。
First American今天发布了2020年第一季度财报。针对DFS指控的听证会定于10月26日举行。
note_en:
In May 2019, KrebsOnSecurity broke the news that the website of mortgage title insurance giant  First American Financial Corp. had exposed approximately 885 million records related to mortgage deals going back to 2003. On Wednesday, regulators in New York announced that First American was the target of their first ever cybersecurity enforcement action in connection with the incident, charges that could bring steep financial penalties.
  Santa Ana, Calif.-based  First American [ NYSE:FAF] is a leading provider of title insurance and settlement services to the real estate and mortgage industries. It employs some 18,000 people and brought in  $6.2 billion in 2019.
 As  first reported here last year, First American’s website exposed 16 years worth of digitized mortgage title insurance records — including bank account numbers and statements, mortgage and tax records, Social Security numbers, wire transaction receipts, and drivers license images.
  According to  a filing (PDF) by the  New York State Department of Financial Services (DFS), the weakness that exposed the documents was first introduced during an application software update in May 2014 and went undetected for years.
 Worse still, the DFS found, the vulnerability was discovered in a penetration test First American conducted on its own in December 2018.
 “Remarkably, Respondent instead allowed unfettered access to the personal and financial data of millions of its customers for six more months until the breach and its serious ramifications were widely publicized by a nationally recognized cybersecurity industry journalist,” the DFS explained in  a statement on the charges.
  Reuters  reports that the penalties could be significant for First American: The DFS considers each instance of exposed personal information a separate violation, and the company faces penalties of up to $1,000 per violation.
 In a written statement, First American said it strongly disagrees with the DFS’s findings, and that its own investigation determined only a “very limited number” of consumers — and none from New York — had personal data accessed without permission.
 In August 2019, the company said a third-party investigation into the exposure  identified just 32 consumers whose non-public personal information likely was accessed without authorization.
 When KrebsOnSecurity asked last year how long it maintained access logs or how far back in time that review went, First American declined to be more specific, saying only that its logs covered a period that was typical for a company of its size and nature.
 But in Wednesday’s filing, the DFS said First American was unable to determine whether records were accessed prior to Jun 2018.
 “Respondent’s forensic investigation relied on a review of web logs retained from June 2018 onward,” the DFS found. “Respondent’s own analysis demonstrated that during this 11-month period, more than 350,000 documents were accessed without authorization by automated ‘bots’ or ‘scraper’ programs designed to collect information on the Internet.
 The records exposed by First American would have been a virtual gold mine for phishers and scammers involved in so-called Business Email Compromise (BEC) scams, which often impersonate real estate agents, closing agencies, title and escrow firms in a bid to  trick property buyers into wiring funds to fraudsters. According to the FBI, BEC scams are the most costly form of cybercrime today.
 First American’s stock price fell more than 6 percent the day after news of their data leak was published here. In the days that followed, the DFS and  U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission each announced they were investigating the company.
 First American released  its first quarter 2020 earnings today. A hearing on the charges alleged by the DFS is slated for Oct. 26.
title: 备案：纽约指控First American Financial因软件更新缺陷导致2019年大规模数据泄露，这是2017年网络安全规则的首次执行
title_en: Filing: NY charges First American Financial over massive 2019 data breach caused by a software update flaw, in the first enforcement of 2017 cybersecurity rules

note:
从某种意义上说，Twitter刚刚度过了一个成功的季度，随着大流行真正站稳脚跟，用户数量大幅飙升。4月至6月，它达到了1.86亿可货币化的日活跃用户(同比增长34%)，但由于广告收入放缓，该季度运营亏损1.24亿美元。
考虑到这一点，该公司表示，它正在寻找广告以外的其他收入来源，其中可能包括某种形式的订阅。然而，它还处于探索这些模式的非常早期的阶段，预计今年不会从其他类型的产品中获得收入。
我们还处于探索增加潜在收入产品的早期阶段，这些产品可以补充我们的广告业务，其中可能包括订阅和其他。现在还为时过早；我们预计2020年不会有任何相对于这些的收入。$TWTR。
-推特投资者关系(@TwitterIR)2020年7月23日。
首席执行官在周四的财报电话会议上(通过美国有线电视新闻网)表示，今年你可能会看到一些不同方法的考验。他指出，当我们要求消费者为Twitter的某些方面付费时，他有一个非常高的门槛。多尔西表示：我们希望确保任何新的收入线都能与我们的广告业务相辅相成。
目前还不清楚Twitter在考虑哪些类型的创收产品和订阅服务。例如，在不做太多猜测的情况下，我们可以看到一个选择，即以月费从平台上删除广告。
note_en:
Twitter just had  a successful quarter in one sense, with a huge surge in user numbers as the pandemic truly took hold. It hit 186 million monetizable daily active users between April and June (up 34 percent year-over-year), but it had an operating loss of $124 million during the quarter as ad revenue slowed.
 With that in mind, the company says it’s looking into other revenue streams beyond advertising, which could include some form of subscriptions. It’s in the very early stages of exploring those models, however, and it doesn’t expect to generate revenue from other types of products this year.
 We're also in early stages of exploring add'l potential revenue products that complement our advertising business, which may include subscriptions & others. It is very early; we do not expect any revenue against these in 2020.  $TWTR
— Twitter Investor Relations (@TwitterIR)  July 23, 2020
 CEO   said on an earnings call Thursday (via  CNN) that "you will likely see some tests this year" of different approaches. He noted that he has "a really high bar for when we would ask consumers to pay for aspects of Twitter,” however. "We want to make sure any new line of revenue is complementary to our advertising business," Dorsey said.
  It’s unclear what types of revenue-driving products and subscription services Twitter has in mind. Without speculating too much, we could see an option to remove ads from the platform for a monthly fee, for instance.
title: 在广告业不景气的情况下，Twitter正在考虑订阅
title_en: Twitter Is Considering Subscriptions Amid An Advertising Slump

控制台模式版本从stdin获取它们的输入，并将它们的输出发送到stdout。可以在常规命令提示符下或在客户端-服务器上下文中运行(例如，通过网络或串行连接，甚至通过互联网)。控制台/终端假定与VT-100兼容，大多数兼容，在这种情况下，大多数标准的BBC Basic VDU命令的行为与预期一致(图形命令除外)，尽管不支持文本视口。或者，这些版本可以用于CGI(通用网关接口)应用程序，或者作为外壳程序的替代品。常用的shell命令可以通过在它们前面加一个星号(*)来访问，这是通常的BBC基本方式。在即时模式下，通过AUTO、DELETE、EDIT、LIST[IF]、LISTO、LOAD、NEW、RENUMBER和SAVE命令提供有限的程序输入和编辑功能，但对于大型程序，建议您使用BBC Basic for Windows或BBC Basic for SDL 2.0 IDE。LOAD命令将以内部(标记化)格式或纯文本文件(必要时将添加行号)加载程序。通过将基本程序指定为命令行参数(它必须是标记化格式)，或者为其指定与可执行文件相同的名称，但扩展名为.bbc，可以自动执行基本程序。控制台模式版本包含适用于相应CPU的汇编程序(在Windows、MacOS和Linux版本中为64位x86汇编程序，在Raspbian版本中为32位ARM汇编程序)。您可以从汇编程序代码和BASIC(使用SYS语句)访问应用程序接口(API)函数，从而允许有经验的程序员生产食品化的应用程序。这些版本是完全免费的，可以为支持的平台下载，如下所示：在每种情况下，zip文件都包含可执行文件、一些库和几个演示BBC Basic程序；内容应该解压到合适的目录。您可能希望将此目录添加到您的路径中，以便可以从任何位置访问可执行文件和库。致命错误返回到立即模式，而不是退出；短文本程序不应被错误识别为标记化。
为单向(客户端→服务器)和往返(服务器→客户端→服务器)通信引入了不同的超时。
LOAD命令现在将以纯文本格式和(#39；Russell&39；)标记化格式加载程序。
note_en:
The Console Mode editions take their input from  stdin and send their output to  stdout somay be run at a regular command prompt or in a client-server context (e.g. through a network or serial connection,or even via the internet). The console/terminal is assumed to be VT-100 compatible, which the great majority are,in which case most of the standard BBC BASIC VDU commands behave as expected (with the exception ofgraphics commands), although text viewports are not supported.Alternatively these editions may be used in CGI ( CommonGateway Interface) applications, or as a shell replacement. Common shell commands may beaccessed by prefixing them with a star (*), in the usual BBC BASIC way. Limited program entry and editing capabilities are provided in Immediate Mode by means of the AUTO, DELETE, EDIT, LIST [IF], LISTO,LOAD, NEW, RENUMBER and SAVE commands, but in the case of large programs you are recommended to use either the BBC BASIC for Windows or  BBC BASIC for SDL 2.0  IDE. The LOAD command will load a program in internal (tokenised)format or as a plain-text file (line numbers will be added if necessary). A BASIC program may be executed automatically by specifyingit as a command-line parameter (it must be in tokenised format) or by giving it the same name as the executable but with a .bbc extension. The   Console Mode editions incorporate an assembler for the appropriate CPU (a 64-bitx86 assembler in the case of the Windows, MacOS and Linux editions, and a 32-bit ARM assembler in thecase of the Raspbian edition). You can access Application Program Interface (API) functions from assembler codeand from BASIC (using the SYS statement), allowing an experienced programmer to producesophisticated applications. These editions are entirely free and may be downloaded for the supported platforms as follows  : In each case the zip file contains the executable, some libraries and a few demonstration BBC BASIC programs; the contents should be extractedto a suitable directory. You may wish to add this directory to your PATH so that the executable and libraries can be accessedfrom 'anywhere'.  'Fatal' errors return to immediate mode rather than quitting; short text programs should not be misidentified as tokenised.
 Introduced different timeouts for one-way (client → server) and round-trip (server → client → server) communication.
  The LOAD command will now load a program in plain-text format as well as in ('Russell') tokenised format.

当大流行关闭了他在俄勒冈州波特兰市中心的办公室时，城市规划师尼尔·海勒(Neil Heller)开始对他过去买下午点心的酒馆感到好奇。如果没有该地区的上班族，没有购物者前往也关闭的大型商店，它可能无法生存。随着许多人继续在家工作-对于一些科技工作者来说，是无限期的-海勒考虑了另一种可能性。一些小企业能搬到居民区吗？
他说：“我想知道这样的人会不会说，‘嘿，我可能会跟着人们去他们的社区。’”“对于依赖上班族的企业来说，现在把办公地点放在人多的地方可能是有利的。”附近的小商店可能也会有更低的租金。
海勒经营着一家名为邻里车间的公司，他是他所谓的ACU或附属商业单位的支持者。如果后院小屋(也被称为ADU，或附属住宅单元)已经成为在现有社区增加新住房的一种越来越常见的方式，那么前面的ACU可以帮助增加新的业务。
这种类型的空间在过去更常见；如果你在一些20世纪20年代的社区走一走，你会看到一家小杂货店，甚至更小的商业空间坐落在其他住宅区，这并不少见。海勒假设，随着汽车在城市中的普及，一些企业选择搬迁到交通最繁忙的街道。分区法也发生了变化。“分区更新变得更加专属住宅，除此之外，变得更加专属独户住宅，”他说。“所以我们差不多把所有东西都划出来了。我们的大多数城市都只是独栋住宅。在我们想要允许的社区范围内，我们已经变得非常、非常短视。“。
为了使这一想法成为可能，分区法律将不得不改变。城市还必须采取措施，使小企业获得使用这些空间的许可变得简单。但是，如果更多以前的ACU被重新激活为小餐馆、咖啡馆或商店-或者如果建造了新的ACU-居民区将受益。海勒说：“调查和民意测验表明，居民们想要当地的便利设施和适合步行的便利设施。”这些建筑可能有助于成为一些规划者所称的15分钟城市的一部分，即所有居民都应该能够在15分钟内步行或骑自行车来满足他们的日常需求。人们可能会少开车。
这一概念甚至可以应用于广阔的郊区。“我认为它们可以用在传统的细分领域，”他说。“传统的、以汽车为中心的细分通常会有很大的挫折。由于各地都在寻找更多适合步行的设施，我认为这将是一种简单的方法，是一种温和的方法，不会太大地改变角色。“
note_en:
When the pandemic closed down his office in downtown Portland, Oregon, urban planner Neil Heller started wondering about the bodega where he used to buy afternoon snacks. Without office workers in the area, and without shoppers headed to larger stores that were also closed, it might not survive. As many people continue working from home—in the case of some tech workers, indefinitely—Heller considered another possibility. Could some small businesses relocate to residential neighborhoods?
  “I wondered if somebody like that might say, ‘Hey, I might follow the people to their neighborhood,'” he says. “It might be advantageous for businesses that rely on office workers to colocate now where the people are.” Small neighborhood shops would likely also have much lower rent.
 Heller, who runs a firm called Neighborhood Workshop, is a proponent of what he calls ACUs, or  accessory commercial units. If backyard cottages (also known as ADUS, or accessory dwelling units) have become an increasingly common way to  add new housing to existing neighborhoods, ACUs in the front could help add new businesses.
 This type of space was more common in the past; if you walk around some 1920s-era neighborhoods, it’s not unusual to see a tiny grocery store or even smaller commercial space sitting on an otherwise residential block. Heller hypothesizes that as cars became widespread in cities, some businesses chose to relocate to streets with the most traffic. Zoning laws also changed. “Zoning updates became more exclusively residential, and beyond that, became more exclusively single-family,” he says. “So we sort of just zoned everything out. Most of our cities are just single-family houses only. We’ve become very, very myopic in our scope of what we want to allow in our neighborhoods.”
 To make the idea possible, zoning laws would have to change. Cities would also have to take steps to make it simple for small businesses to get permits to use the spaces. But if more former ACUs are reactivated as tiny restaurants or coffee shops or stores—or if new ACUs were built—residential neighborhoods would benefit. “Surveys and polls suggest that residents want local amenities and walkable amenities,” Heller says. The buildings could help be part of what some planners call the  15-minute city, the idea that all residents should be able to walk or bike to take care of their daily needs within 15 minutes. People would likely drive less.
 The concept could be applied even in sprawling suburbs. “I think they could be used in conventional subdivisions,” he says. “A conventional, auto-centric subdivision will typically have large setbacks. As places are looking for more walkable amenities, I think that this would be an easy approach, and one that would be a gentle approach and wouldn’t change the character very much.”
title: 把小企业重新搬回居民区
title_en: Put tiny businesses back into residential neighborhoods

史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克(Steve Wozniak)起诉YouTube允许骗子在虚假的比特币赠品中使用他的名字和肖像。
根据向加利福尼亚州高等法院提起的诉讼，骗子一直在该平台上发布视频，声称沃兹尼亚克正在主持比特币促销活动。他们让用户相信，如果他们把比特币寄到提供的地址，“沃兹尼亚克”将返还两倍的金额。
起诉书中写道：“YouTube上有源源不断的诈骗视频和促销活动，这些视频虚假使用原告史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克(Steve Wozniak)和其他著名科技企业家的图片和视频，骗取了YouTube用户数百万美元。”
这起诉讼包括沃兹尼亚克“5000-BTC”和“10000-BTC”赠品的广告视频截图，其中包括这位苹果联合创始人的图片。似乎也有一些视频使用了其他科技领袖的肖像，包括埃隆·马斯克、比尔·盖茨和金融自助大师罗伯特·清崎。
原告包括沃兹尼亚克和其他17个人，他们声称YouTube知道这些骗局，但仍然没有删除这些视频。
起诉书中写道：“尽管原告和其他用户一再恳求YouTube及时采取行动结束这一比特币赠品骗局，但YouTube一再推迟或拒绝这样做。”“由于被告在宣传这一犯罪企业方面的严重失败和肯定的不当行为，原告沃兹尼亚克的声誉已经并将继续遭受不可弥补的损害，包括原告在内的YouTube用户被诈骗了数百万美元。除了其他救济措施外，原告还寻求一项命令，要求YouTube最终结束其主持、推广这些具有刑事欺诈性的视频和促销活动，并从中获利的无耻做法。
YouTube的一位发言人在给The Verge的一份声明中表示：“我们认真对待滥用我们平台的行为，当我们发现有人违反我们的政策时，会迅速采取行动，比如诈骗或冒充。”
欺诈性“赠品”长期以来一直是比特币世界的一个特征，但在Twitter遭到全网黑客攻击后，它们正得到更多关注。黑客利用账户访问进行了大规模的诈骗。上周，黑客侵入了一些知名用户的账户，包括巴拉克·奥巴马(Barack Obama)、乔·拜登(Joe Biden)、埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)、比尔·盖茨(Bill Gates)、坎耶·韦斯特(Kanye West)和迈克尔·布隆伯格(Michael Bloomberg)。黑客利用被攻破的账户宣布欺诈性的赠品，声称将比特币发送到提供的地址的用户将获得两倍的回报。据信，这一骗局为其创造者赚取了近12万美元。(YouTube骗局虽然目标也是比特币所有者，但似乎并不涉及泄露的账户信息。)
Twitter在开始大约一小时后就承认了这一情况，并在其支持账号上发推文称，“我们正在调查并采取措施修复它。”在调查这起事件期间，该公司最终锁定了所有经过验证的账户，不能发布推文，这一措施已经实施了两个多小时。
Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)在晚间晚些时候写道：“我们都对发生这样的事情感到难过。”“我们正在进行诊断，当我们对到底发生了什么有了更完整的了解后，我们将尽我们所能分享一切。”
note_en:
Steve Wozniak is  suing YouTube for allowing scammers to use his name and likeness in phony bitcoin giveaways.
 According to the  lawsuit, filed in the Superior Court of the State of California, crooks have been posting videos on the platform claiming that Wozniak is hosting a bitcoin promotion. They convince users that if they send bitcoin to a provided address, “Wozniak” will return double the amount.
 “YouTube has featured a steady stream of scam videos and promotions that falsely use images and videos of Plaintiff Steve Wozniak, and other famous tech entrepreneurs, and that have defrauded YouTube users out of millions of dollars,” the complaint reads.
  The lawsuit includes screenshots of videos advertising “5,000-BTC” and “10,000-BTC” giveaways from Wozniak, which include images of the Apple co-founder. There appear to be videos using the likeness of other tech leaders as well, including Elon Musk, Bill Gates, and financial self-help guru Robert Kiyosaki.
 The plaintiffs, which include Wozniak and 17 other individuals, allege that YouTube is aware of these scams but has nonetheless not taken the videos down.
  “Despite Plaintiffs’ and legions of other users’ repeated pleas that YouTube take timely action to end this bitcoin giveaway scam, YouTube repeatedly delayed or refused to do so,” reads the lawsuit. “As a result of defendants’ egregious failures to act and affirmative misconduct in promoting this criminal enterprise, Plaintiff Wozniak has suffered, and continues to suffer, irreparable harm to his reputation, and YouTube users, including plaintiffs, have been defrauded out of millions of dollars. Among other relief, Plaintiffs seek an order requiring YouTube to finally end its outrageous practice of hosting, promoting, and profiting from these criminally fraudulent videos and promotions.”
 “We take abuse of our platform seriously, and take action quickly when we detect violations of our policies, such as scams or impersonation,” said a YouTube spokesperson in a statement to  The Verge.
 Fraudulent “giveaways” have long been a feature of the bitcoin world, but they are getting more attention after a network-wide hack on Twitter that leveraged account access to perform the scam on a massive scale.  Last week, hackers accessed the accounts of a number of high-profile users, including Barack Obama, Joe Biden, Elon Musk, Bill Gates, Kanye West, and Michael Bloomberg. Hackers used the breached accounts to announce fraudulent giveaways, claiming users who sent bitcoin to a provided address would receive double the amount in return. The scam is believed to have earned its creators nearly $120,000. (The Youtube scam, while also targeting bitcoin owners, does not appear to involve compromised account information.)
  Twitter acknowledged that situation about an hour after it began, tweeting from its support handle, “We are investigating and taking steps to fix it.” The company eventually locked all verified accounts from tweeting while it looked into the incident, a measure that was in place for over two hours.
 “We all feel terrible this happened,” Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey wrote later in the evening. “We’re diagnosing and will share everything we can when we have a more complete understanding of exactly what happened.”
title: 苹果联合创始人史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克起诉YouTube
title_en: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak is suing YouTube

note:
华盛顿(路透社)-美国四家最大的科技公司的首席执行官计划在下周的国会听证会上转移批评，称他们自己面临竞争，并驳斥了他们如此占据主导地位的说法，以转移对他们利用市场力量伤害竞争对手的批评。
Facebook、亚马逊(Amazon.com Inc.)、Alphabet旗下的谷歌(Google)和苹果(Apple)的首席执行官将于7月27日在众议院司法委员会的反垄断小组面前发表讲话。据知情人士透露，他们将在网上提交证词。
作为调查的一部分，该小组正在询问这些公司，调查它们是否积极努力伤害和消除规模较小的竞争对手，同时并不总是为客户做出最佳选择。
这场备受瞩目的听证会将汇集亚马逊的杰夫·贝佐斯、Facebook的马克·扎克伯格、苹果的蒂姆·库克和谷歌的桑达尔·皮查伊，这将是美国对大型科技公司日益强烈的反弹的关键时刻，可能会在两党的高管和持怀疑态度的议员之间建立一场对峙。
然而，许多科技游说团体表示，听证会不太可能解决核心反垄断问题，也不太可能带来新的信息。
苹果在面临伤害新来者的批评后，可能会被问及其应用商店的管理方式。苹果公司告诉路透社，它将辩称自己没有控制应用程序的市场份额。这家iPhone制造商将其商店视为一项旨在确保其手机安全性和可靠性的功能。
苹果将解决一些问题，比如应用商店的审批程序-长期以来，这一直是开发者的痛处，他们表示，他们的应用程序在没有警告的情况下被搁置-以及有关苹果没有共享手机位置数据等关键功能的指控。
一位消息人士表示，亚马逊的杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)可能会谈论消费者在网上购物的选择，以及冠状病毒大流行如何推动整个电子商务-包括沃尔玛(Walmart)等大型零售竞争对手。
这位消息人士补充说，预计他还将谈论其第三方市场平台上的小卖家，以及“他们如何在来自亚马逊的竞争中继续蓬勃发展”。亚马逊因如何利用来自小卖家的数据让自己的业务受益而受到密切关注。
消息人士称，贝佐斯还可能通过限制小卖家出售的库存来解决有关该公司利用大流行的指控，但将避免提出有争议的问题，如围绕拆分公司的对话。
另一位消息人士称，Facebook的扎克伯格将采取类似的策略。预计他将辩称，该公司拥有强大的竞争对手，包括广告方面的谷歌和亚马逊，以及社交媒体领域的Twitter和TikTok。
预计扎克伯格将再次呼吁Facebook在社交媒体中的有害内容、选举诚信和隐私等领域进行政府监管，这些领域是该公司受到批评的领域。
目前尚不清楚谷歌可能提出的论点的细节。但最近几周，该公司发布了博客文章和一份白皮书，声称它仍然面临着大量的竞争，向广告买家和卖家收取的费用是合理的。
“在安抚反科技的批评者方面，科技公司的首席执行官们所能做的并不多……。这场听证会不是关于寻找真相，而是创造新闻故事，“行业游说团体NetChoice的副总裁兼总法律顾问卡尔·萨博(Carl Szabo)说。
哈佛大学肯尼迪学院(Harvard Kennedy School)肖伦斯坦媒体、政治和公共政策中心(Shorenstein Center on Media，Politics and Public Policy)研究员迪帕扬·戈什(Dipayan Ghosh)在国会论坛(Capitol Forum)组织的一次采访中表示，美国没有针对大型科技公司使用的做法的监管规定，听证会是朝着这个方向迈出的关键一步。
note_en:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) - The chief executives of four of the largest U.S. tech companies plan to deflect criticism next week in a Congressional hearing into their use of market power to hurt rivals by saying they themselves face competition and by debunking claims they are so dominant.
  The CEOs of Facebook, Amazon.com Inc, Alphabet’s Google and Apple, are set to speak before the House Judiciary Committee’s antitrust panel on July 27. They will present their testimonies virtually, according to sources familiar with their plans.
 The panel is questioning the companies as part of its probe into whether they actively work to harm and eliminate smaller rivals, while not always making the best choices for their customers.
 The high-profile hearing, which will bring together Amazon’s Jeff Bezos, Facebook’s Mark Zuckerberg, Apple’s Tim Cook and Google’s Sundar Pichai, will be a key moment in the growing backlash against Big Tech in the United States and is likely to set up a face-off between the executives and skeptical lawmakers from both parties.
 Many tech lobbying groups say the hearing is unlikely to address core antitrust issues or bring new information to the table, however.
 Apple is likely to be quizzed about the way it manages its app store after facing criticisms it hurts newcomers. Apple told Reuters it will argue it does not have controlling market share for apps. The iPhone maker views its store as a feature designed to ensure the security and reliability of its phones.
 Apple will address issues such as the approval process for the app store - long a sore point with developers who have said their apps are held up without warning - and allegations it does not share key functions such as data about the phone’s location.
  A source said Amazon’s Jeff Bezos is likely to talk about the options consumers have for online purchases and how the coronavirus pandemic has boosted e-commerce overall - including for large retail rivals such as Walmart.
 He is also expected to talk about small sellers on its third-party marketplace platform and “how they have continued to thrive despite competition from Amazon,” the source added. Amazon has come under scrutiny on how it uses data from small sellers to benefit its own business.
 Bezos could also address allegations the company took advantage of the pandemic by limiting inventory sold by small sellers but will stay away from bringing up contentious issues such as the conversation around breaking up the company, the source said.
 Facebook’s Zuckerberg will follow a similar tack, another source said. He is expected to argue that the company has strong competitors, including Google and Amazon on the advertising side and Twitter and TikTok in social media.
 Zuckerberg is expected to renew Facebook’s call for government regulation in areas such as harmful content in social media, election integrity, and privacy - areas where the company has been criticized.
 Details of Google’s likely arguments were not available. But in recent weeks the firm has published blog posts and a white paper asserting that it still faces plenty of competition and that the fees it charges ad buyers and sellers are justified.
   “There’s not much tech CEOs can do to appease anti-tech critics... this hearing is not about finding truth but creating news stories,” said Carl Szabo, vice president and general counsel at industry lobby group NetChoice.
  The U.S. does not have regulations to deal with the practices employed by large tech companies and the hearing is a key step in that direction, Dipayan Ghosh, a fellow at the Shorenstein Center on Media, Politics and Public Policy at the Harvard Kennedy School said during an interview organized by the Capitol Forum.

政治原定由众议院司法反垄断小组委员会举行的听证会可能会推迟，以便让立法者出席众议员约翰·刘易斯的葬礼。
据两位知情人士透露，周一与杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)、马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)、蒂姆·库克(Tim Cook)和桑达尔·皮查伊(Sundar Pichai)举行的轰动一时的国会听证会可能会推迟。
听证会原定于众议院司法反垄断小组委员会举行，但可能会推迟，以便让立法者出席众议员约翰·刘易斯(John Lewis)的葬礼。刘易斯，一位受人爱戴的民权领袖，被称为美国国会的良知，于7月20日去世，享年80岁。他在华盛顿举行葬礼的日期尚未正式宣布。
Facebook、谷歌(Google)、亚马逊(Amazon)和苹果(Apple)的高管几乎将在小组委员会面前作证，内容是他们庞大的业务是否压垮了竞争对手，并不公平地使他们的竞争对手处于不利地位。
note_en:
Politics   The hearing, which was set to be held by the House Judiciary antitrust subcommittee, could be postponed to allow lawmakers to attend Rep. John Lewis' funeral.
     Monday's blockbuster congressional hearing with Jeff Bezos, Mark Zuckerberg, Tim Cook and Sundar Pichai is likely to be postponed, according to two sources familiar with the matter.
 The hearing, which was set to be held by the House Judiciary antitrust subcommittee, could be postponed in order to allow lawmakers to attend Rep. John Lewis' funeral. Lewis, a beloved civil rights leader known as the "conscience of the U.S. Congress," died at age 80 on July 20. The date of his funeral in Washington has not been officially announced.
  The top executives of Facebook, Google, Amazon and Apple were  set to testify virtually before the subcommittee about whether their enormous businesses have crushed rivals and unfairly disadvantaged their competition.
title: 消息人士：众议院周一与大型科技公司执行长举行的反垄断听证会可能推迟，以便让议员出席众议员约翰·刘易斯的葬礼
title_en: Sources: House's antitrust hearing with big tech CEOs on Monday is likely to be postponed in order to allow lawmakers to attend Rep. John Lewis' funeral

跳转到导航搜索罗伯特·安东·威尔逊(原名罗伯特·爱德华·威尔逊；1932年1月18日至2007年1月11日)是一位美国作家、小说家、散文家、编辑、剧作家、诗人、未来学家和自称不可知论的神秘主义者。威尔逊被不和谐主义公认为爱泼斯可波斯、教皇和圣人，他通过他的著作和采访帮助宣传这个团体。
威尔逊将他的工作描述为试图打破条件性的联想，以一种新的方式看待世界，许多模型被认为是模型或地图，没有一个模型被提升为真理。[1]他的目标是试图让人们进入广义不可知论的状态，不只是对上帝的不可知论，而是对一切的不可知论。
威尔逊是反主流文化中的重要人物，可以与他的合著者之一蒂莫西·利里(Timothy Leary)以及特伦斯·麦肯纳(Terence McKenna)等人相提并论。[3]。
这个词的愚蠢之处一直萦绕在我的脑海中。(#34；&#34；is.&#34；&#34；is&#34；is。如果它被废除，人类的思想可能会开始有意义。我不知道什么是什么，我只知道这一刻在我看来是什么样子。
他原名罗伯特·爱德华·威尔逊(Robert Edward Wilson)，出生于纽约布鲁克林的卫理公会医院(Methodist Hospital)，在弗拉特布什度过了他的头几年，大约在四五岁的时候和家人搬到了下层中产阶级格里森海滩(Gerritsen Beach)，在那里他们一直住在那里，直到威尔逊13岁时搬到坚定的中产阶级社区湾岭(Bay Ridge)。他小时候患有小儿麻痹症，并发现用肯尼方法(由伊丽莎白·肯尼创造)治疗通常有效，但美国医学会当时拒绝了这种方法。小儿麻痹症的影响一直伴随着威尔逊一生，通常表现为轻微的肌肉痉挛，导致他偶尔使用拐杖，直到2000年，他经历了一场脊髓灰质炎后综合症的重大比赛，一直持续到他去世。
威尔逊就读于天主教文法学校，很可能是与格里森海滩的复活教会有关的学校[需要引用]，并就读于布鲁克林技术高中(一所精选的公立学校)，以摆脱天主教的影响；在布鲁克林理工学院，威尔逊受到了文学现代主义(特别是埃兹拉·庞德和詹姆斯·乔伊斯)、西方哲学传统、当时的创新历史学家如查尔斯·A·比尔德、科幻小说(包括奥拉夫·斯塔夫的作品)的影响；威尔逊受到了文学现代主义(特别是埃兹拉·庞德和詹姆斯·乔伊斯)、西方哲学传统、当时的创新历史学家如查尔斯·A·比尔德(Charles A.Beard)、科幻小说(包括奥拉夫·斯塔夫的作品)的影响。[4]他后来会回想起，这家人生活得非常好。与这一时期的大萧条相比，我以为我们终于成了花边窗帘的爱尔兰人。
1950年毕业后，威尔逊从事过一系列工作(包括救护车司机、工程助理、推销员和医务兵)，在业余时间写作时吸收了各种哲学家和文化实践(包括贝波普、精神分析、伯特兰·罗素、卡尔·荣格、威廉·赖克、利昂·托洛茨基和艾恩·兰德，后来他拒绝了这些人)。1952年至1957年，他在布鲁克林理工学院学习电气工程和数学，1957年至1958年，他在纽约大学学习英语教育，但这两个机构都未能获得学位。[4][6][7]。
在吸食大麻近十年后，他于1961年12月28日首次在俄亥俄州黄泉市试验美斯卡林。[4]威尔逊在20世纪50年代末开始担任自由撰稿人和广告文案。他用外祖父的名字安东来写作，告诉自己他要拯救爱德华，因为当他写“伟大的美国小说”时，后来发现罗伯特·安东·威尔逊已经成为一个既定的身份。
1962年，他担任俄亥俄州布鲁克维尔生活学校(School For Living)旗下平衡生活杂志(Balance Living)的联合主编，并短暂回到纽约，担任拉尔夫·金兹堡(Ralph Ginzburg)季刊的副主编。之后，他离开花花公子，于1965年至1971年在那里担任副主编。根据威尔逊的说法，“花花公子”付给我的薪水比我工作过的任何其他杂志都要高，从来没有想过我会成为一名墨守成规的人，或者出卖自己的灵魂作为回报。我在兔子帝国的日子过得很愉快。我是在40岁时辞职的，我觉得如果最后不努力全职写作，我就不能忍受自己。[5]威尔逊和经常合作者罗伯特·谢伊(Robert Shea)一起编辑了该杂志的“花花公子论坛”(Playboy Forum)，这是一个信件栏目，由对“花花公子”哲学社论专栏的回复组成。[5]威尔逊和经常合作者罗伯特·谢伊(Robert Shea)一起编辑了该杂志的“花花公子论坛”(Playboy Forum)。在此期间，他在艾伦·瓦茨的“现实主义”一书的鼓动下，报道了蒂莫西·利里(Timothy Leary)和理查德·阿尔伯特(Richard Alpert)在纽约米尔布鲁克的卡斯特利亚基金会(Castalia Foundation)，与威廉·S·巴罗斯(William S.Burroughs)和艾伦·金斯伯格(Allen Ginsberg)培养了重要的友谊，并于1965年在纽约自由大学(Free University Of New York)讲授无政府主义和协同政治学(Anarchist And Synergetic Politics)。[8]。
他从派迪亚大学(Paideia University)获得心理学学士、硕士和博士学位(1978年)和博士学位(1981年)，这是一所未经认证的机构，现已关闭。[9][10][11]威尔逊修改了他的论文，并在1983年出版了“普罗米修斯崛起”。[12]。
威尔逊于1958年与自由撰稿人兼诗人阿伦·莱利(Arlen Riley)结婚。[12]他们有四个孩子，包括克里斯蒂娜·威尔逊·皮尔森和帕特里夏·露娜·威尔逊。1976年，15岁的露娜在她工作的商店被殴打致死，当时她15岁，成为第一个被湾区冷冻学会保存大脑的人。[13]阿伦·莱利·威尔逊在一系列中风后于1999年5月22日去世。[14][15]。
在威尔逊的35本书和许多其他作品中，也许他最著名的书仍然是邪教经典系列“光照者！三部曲(1975)，与谢伊合著。这三本书--“金字塔里的眼睛”、“金苹果”和“利维坦”--很快就成了一本书，哲学上和幽默地审视了许多主题，其中包括神秘而神奇的象征主义和历史、20世纪60年代的反文化、秘密社团、有关作家H.P.洛夫克拉夫特和作家兼神秘的亚历斯特·克劳利的数据，以及美国人对阴谋论和阴谋论的偏执。这本书意在取笑阴谋论的心境。[19]。
威尔逊和谢伊在担任“花花公子”杂志论坛主编期间，从寄给“花花公子”杂志的信件中获得了许多奇怪的材料。[20]这些书将真实信息与富有想象力的虚构融合在一起，让读者参与到威尔逊所说的游击本体论中，显然他在《光明录》中将其称为思想他妈的行动！这部三部曲还勾勒出一套自由意志主义和无政府主义的公理，被称为席琳的法则(以“光明录”中的一个人物哈巴德·席琳命名)，威尔逊在其他著作中多次重提这些概念。
在光明会的众多次要情节中！一本书讲述了生物战和推翻“美国人权法案”(United States Bill Of Rights)，另一本书详细描述了约翰·F·肯尼迪(John F.Kennedy)遇刺事件(至少有五名狙击手，他们都为不同的事业工作，准备射杀肯尼迪)，这本书的高潮发生在一场摇滚音乐会上，观众集体面临成为大规模人类牺牲品的危险。
光明会！流行的不和谐主义和术语Fnord&34；的使用。它融合了受威廉·S·巴罗斯、詹姆斯·乔伊斯和埃兹拉·庞德等作家影响的实验性散文风格。[21]虽然谢伊和威尔逊再也没有进行过如此规模的合作，但威尔逊继续扩展《光明会》的主题！在他的写作生涯中，他一直在阅读书籍。他后来的大部分小说都包含了“性魔术师”(威尔逊的第一部小说，写于“光明会！”发行之前，其中包括了他的许多相同的人物)和“光明会！”中的交叉人物。三部曲。
光明会！曾获普罗米修斯名人堂最佳经典小说奖，1986年被自由意志主义未来主义者协会评选为科幻小说，拥有多个国际版本，并在肯·坎贝尔将其制作为10小时的戏剧时发现了对舞台的改编。它还出现在史蒂夫·杰克逊游戏公司(Steve Jackson Games)的两款纸牌游戏中，其中一款是交易纸牌游戏(光照派：世界新秩序)。Eye N Apple Productions和Rip Off Press制作了这部三部曲的漫画版。
威尔逊又写了两部受欢迎的小说系列。第一本是后来以单卷出版的三部曲，是薛定谔的“猫”。第二本是“历史光明会编年史”，共出版了三本。在出版这两部三部曲之间，威尔逊出版了一部独立的小说，“光明会的面具”(1981年)，由于主人公的血统，这部小说符合“历史光明会编年史”的时间线，虽然早些时候出版了，但有资格进入该系列的第四卷。
薛定谔的猫由三卷组成：“隔壁的宇宙”、“帽子把戏”和“信鸽”。威尔逊将这三本书设定在不同的另类世界，大多数人物几乎保持相同，但可能有不同的名字、职业和背景故事。这些书涵盖了量子力学领域，以及科学中存在的各种哲学和解释。这本书将自己描述为一本神奇的教科书和一种印心。单卷本省略了许多整页，与原来的分册相比，还有许多其他的遗漏。
由寡妇之子(1982)、自然之神(1991)和寡妇之子(1982)组成的“历史光明会编年史”遵循了几个人物跨越不同世代、不同时期和不同国家的时间线。这些书涵盖了许多其他主题，包括光照派和相关团体的历史、遗产和仪式。
光照派的面具，以虚构的背景中的历史人物为特色，包含了大量神秘的数据。这本书混合了阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦、詹姆斯·乔伊斯、亚历斯特·克劳利、西格蒙德·弗洛伊德、卡尔·荣格、弗拉基米尔·伊里奇·列宁和其他人，专注于潘和其他神秘的偶像、思想和实践。这本书包括克劳利和乔伊斯的生平和作品中的致敬、恶搞和贴纸。
威尔逊的戏剧“地狱中的威廉·赖克”于1987年出版成书，并首次在都柏林埃德蒙·伯克剧院、旧金山和洛杉矶演出。它以许多真实和虚构的人物为特色，包括玛丽莲·梦露、山姆大叔和威廉·赖克本人。威尔逊还撰写并出版了两部尚未制作的剧本：现实是你可以逃脱惩罚的：插图剧本(1992)和墙倒塌(1997)。
威尔逊的书“宇宙触发器I：光照派的最终秘密”已经被改编成戏剧舞台剧，由黛西·厄里斯·坎贝尔改编成舞台剧，她是英国戏剧特立独行的肯·坎贝尔的女儿，她上演了“光明会！1977年在皇家国家剧院演出。[12]该剧于2014年11月23日在利物浦首演，然后转移到伦敦和布莱顿。[24]一些费用是通过众筹支付的。[25]威尔逊的书本身就是献给肯·坎贝尔和英国利物浦科幻剧院的。
在他的非虚构和部分自传性的“宇宙触发器I：光照派的最终秘密”(1977)及其两部续集中，以及在许多其他作品中，威尔逊考察了共济会、不和谐主义、苏菲主义、光照派、未来学、禅宗佛教、丹尼斯和特伦斯·麦肯纳、杰克·帕森斯、阿莱斯特·克劳利和G.I.葛吉夫的神秘实践、瑜伽和许多其他深奥的或反文化的东西。
威尔逊提倡蒂莫西·利里的意识和神经体/语言工程的8回路模型，他在许多书中都提到了这一点，包括“普罗米修斯崛起”(1983年，1997年修订)和“量子心理学”(1990年)，这两本书包含了一些实用技术，旨在帮助读者摆脱现实隧道。与Leary一起，他帮助推广了未来主义的太空迁移、智力增长和寿命延长的想法，这些想法结合在一起形成了单词符号Smi²le。
威尔逊1986年出版的“新宗教裁判所”一书认为，无论现实由什么组成，实际上看起来都比我们通常想象的要怪异得多。它引用了贝尔定理和阿兰对贝尔定理的实验证明等来源，以表明主流科学具有强烈的唯物主义偏见，而且实际上现代物理学可能已经反驳了唯物主义形而上学。
威尔逊还支持巴克明斯特·富勒的工作和乌托邦理论，并考察了查尔斯·福特的理论。他和罗伦·科尔曼成了朋友，就像他与媒体理论家马歇尔·麦克卢汉(Marshall McLuhan)和神经语言编程(Neuro Language Programming)联合创始人理查德·班德勒(Richard Bandler)一样，他和他们一起教授研讨会。他还钦佩詹姆斯·乔伊斯，并在1988年出版的“巧合：头部测试”一书中对詹姆斯·乔伊斯和乔伊斯的两部小说“芬尼根的守灵”和“尤利西斯”写了大量评论。[28][28]。
虽然威尔逊经常讽刺和批评一些新时代的信仰，但专门销售新时代材料的书店经常出售他的书。威尔逊是神秘界和新异教徒圈子里的知名作家，他在1981年的小说“光照派的面具”中使用了阿莱斯特·克劳利作为主角，在他的小说中也加入了H·P·洛夫克拉夫特作品中的一些元素，有时还声称自己察觉到了与魔法实体的接触(当被问及这些实体是否看起来真实时，他回答说它们看起来足够真实，尽管也很真实。)(当被问及这些实体看起来是否真实时，他回答说，它们看起来足够真实，尽管它们看起来足够真实。)(当被问及这些实体看起来是否真实时，他回答说，它们看起来足够真实，尽管它们看起来足够真实。)威尔逊是神秘界和新异教徒圈子里的知名作家。更容易摆脱，后来我断定他的经历可能来自于我的右脑和左脑的对话。)。[29]他告诫初学者不要使用神秘的练习，因为仓促进行这样的练习以及由此释放的能量可能会导致人们完全发疯。[30][30]。
威尔逊还批评了信仰体系过于僵化的科学型，将他们等同于宗教原教旨主义者的狂热。在1988年的一次采访中，当被问及他最新出版的书“新宗教裁判所：非理性理性主义和科学堡垒”时，威尔逊评论道：
我创造了非理性理性主义这个词，因为那些人声称自己是理性主义者，但他们却受到如此多禁忌的支配。他们是如此的恐惧，如此的敌意，如此的狭隘，如此的惊恐，如此的紧张和教条。我写这本书是因为我厌倦了讽刺原教旨主义基督教。我决定改变一下讽刺原教旨主义的唯物主义，因为两者同样滑稽……。唯物主义原教旨主义者比基督教原教旨主义者更有趣，因为他们认为自己是理性的！他们从不怀疑任何事情，除了他们有偏见的东西。他们中没有一个人对AMA，或者对建制科学中的任何东西，或者任何根深蒂固的教条说过任何怀疑的话。他们只是对吓到他们的新想法持怀疑态度。他们实际上是教条地致力于他们在大学里学到的东西。...[31]。
在2003年接受“High Times”杂志采访时，威尔逊称自己是模特不可知论者，他说。
包括从不100%相信或100%否认任何宇宙模型或地图。跟随科兹布斯基，我把事情放在概率上，而不是绝对上...。我唯一的创新之处在于将这种探索性的态度从最硬的硬科学，物理学，应用到软科学，然后应用到非科学领域，如政治，意识形态，陪审团裁决，当然还有阴谋论。[32][font=宋体]。
威尔逊在“宇宙触发：第一卷”中声称不要相信任何事情，因为信仰是智力的死亡。[33]他把这种方法描述为“也许是逻辑”。
威尔逊在赛博朋克杂志Mondo 2000的文章中写到了这个和其他话题。[34][34]。
威尔逊倾向于一种基本的收入保障形式；在首字母缩写RICH下综合了几个想法。他的观点在“光明会”报纸上的“富裕的经济”一文中得到了阐述。[35]在一篇批评资本主义的文章中，威尔逊自称是自由意志主义社会主义者，他说，我只向怀疑论者提一件事：请不要提起苏联。国家资本主义的那个可怕例子与我和其他自由意志主义社会主义者作为现行体制的替代方案毫无关系。到了20世纪80年代，他对社会主义的标签不那么热衷了，他在“普罗米修斯崛起”(Prometheus Rise)中写道，他不喜欢社会主义的蔓延。[37]在他的《就在你现在坐的地方》一书中，他称赞了地质学家经济学家西尔维奥·格塞尔(Silvio Gesell)。[38]在《左右：非欧几里德视角》一文中，威尔逊称赞了几个被排除在外的中间人，这些人超越了垄断资本主义和极权社会主义之间陈词滥调的争论；他说他最喜欢的是本杰明·塔克和皮埃尔-约瑟夫·普罗德洪的互助主义无政府主义，但他也对格塞尔、亨利·乔治、C.H.道格拉斯和巴克克的观点给予了好评。他说，他最喜欢的是本杰明·塔克(Benjamin Tucker)和皮埃尔-约瑟夫·普罗德洪(Pierre-Joseph Proudhon)的互惠无政府主义，但他也对格塞尔、亨利·乔治、C·H·道格拉斯和巴克克的观点给予了好评。[39]威尔逊还认为自己是一个无政府主义者，并将他的信仰体系描述为塔克、斯普纳、富勒、庞德、亨利·乔治、罗斯巴德、道格拉斯、科日布斯基、普罗德洪和马克思的混合体。威尔逊曾在美国自由党的大会上多次发言。他将本杰明·塔克的“进步与贫困”、亨利·乔治的“进步与贫困”和格塞尔的“自然经济秩序”等书列入了20本推荐书的名单中，这些都是每个人在明智地谈论21世纪之前真正需要咀嚼和消化的最起码的书籍。[41]他把本杰明·塔克的“进步与贫困”，以及格塞尔的“自然经济秩序”列在了20本推荐书中，这些都是每个人真正需要咀嚼和消化的最起码的东西，然后才能明智地谈论21世纪。
罗伯特·安东·威尔逊(Robert Anton Wilson)和他的妻子阿伦·莱利·威尔逊(Arlen Riley Wilson)于1975年创立了人类未来研究所。
从1982年到去世，威尔逊一直与意识探索协会有业务关系，该协会主持了他与老朋友蒂莫西·利里(Timothy Leary)的第一次台上对话，书名为“内边疆”(The Internal Frontier)[42]。[43][44]威尔逊将他的新书《新宗教裁判所》献给A.C.E.的联席董事杰夫·罗森鲍姆和约瑟夫·罗森伯格。
威尔逊还加入了亚天才教会，后者称他为教皇鲍勃。[46]他为他们的文学做出了贡献，包括《鲍勃的三个拳头的故事》一书，并多次与他们的创始人伊万·斯坦牧师同台演出。威尔逊还创立了枪械和兴奋剂党(Guns And Dope Party)。[47]。
作为完全知情的陪审团协会顾问委员会的成员，威尔逊致力于告知公众陪审团无效，即陪审员废除他们认为不公正的法律的权利。[48]他倡导并撰写了关于E-Prime的文章，E-Prime是一种英语形式，没有动词的所有形式是&#34；(例如&#34；is&#34；，&#34；are&#34；，&#34；are&#34；，等等)。(#34；is&#34；，&#34；are&#34；，&#34；are&#34；，等等)。[49][49]。
作为一名数十年的毒品研究人员，威尔逊强烈反对他所说的打击某些毒品的战争，他作为特邀嘉宾参加了为期一周的1999年在阿姆斯特丹举行的年度大麻杯(Cannabis Cup)，[50]他使用并经常推广医用大麻的使用。[51]2002年，他参加了圣克鲁斯Wo/Men医用大麻联盟组织的抗议活动。[52][font=宋体]。
2006年6月22日，赫芬顿邮报
